title: Was Shinjis childhood as secluded as Reis
display_title: Was Shinjis childhood as secluded as Reis
wikitext:
[]: Was Shinji's childhood as secluded as Rei's?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 11, 2013 1:51 pm Okay, that's something that has been nagging my mind for a while : what kind of life Shinji lived before arriving in Tokyo-3, the ten years he lived with his "teacher"? After looking at the trilogy again, I noticed some details about Shinji that could hint that he actually lived a very secluded live, not unlike Rei's life prior to the events of the movies. 1/ Shinji is strangely accustomed to live alone : In 1.0 when Misato learn that Shinji will live alone in the Geofront, she oppose the decision as he's just a 14 years old kid. But Shinji reassures her by saying that she shouldn't worry, that he's accustomed to live alone... wait, what? Shinji obviously treat the situation in ends into(living with Misato) as something new for him, but wouldn't that situation be very similar to the one he was before, living with a legal guardian who aren't related by blood with him? Is that teacher absent that often from the house that it's like Shinji was living alone most of the time? Well, he certainly have some household skills vitals for someone living alone : he washes the dishes and completely cleans the apartment soon after arriving, probably make the laundry too(meaning that he also know how to iron the laundry), and he knows how to cook, but watch out, it's not just knowing how to cook common dishes : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 11, 2013 2:10 pm I definitely think NGE Gendo's case was one of neglect and fear, so there's not much to be said there. NME really does take things a step further through flat out manipulation. Also don't forget in 1.0 where Fuyutsuki and Gendo have an exchange that Gendo was planning his scenario for Shinji and Rei over the past 14 years! By the way, thanks for making a point of keeping NGE and NME separate continuities in your analysis; folks tend to forget about that. Of course, we really don't know exactly what NME-Gendo's end-goal is with manipulating Shinji/Rei/Eva-01: it could be different from NGE, in one way or another. I personally think it'd be another great way to swerve the original NGE audience, just like how 2.0 and 3.0 did.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 11, 2013 4:35 pm Shinji himself explicitly noted in that same scene that he had to aquire these cooking skills after moving in with Misato ( and there's nothing to indicate that this wasn't the same with the rest of his household skills) Shinji's a fast learner, especially the Rebuild version. You can very much expect a 14 year old (Especially when they live with only one adult who also has to do something for a living and is probably out working a lot simply because that's how it works, not because he's neglectful. While Gendo probably pays him a nifty sum for Shinji's expenses, he probably also taught other kids in some fashion - Shinji's level of self-sufience isn't that outrageous when you compare him to other kids with single parents, parents who both work, or have numerous younger siblings) to clean their own room himself and help a little in the household, and since he's got this complex in his head that he's unwanted on this world, he'd probably offer do do stuff himself where he can. This "not wanting to be a burden" to the teacher guy/being afraid that any adult figure may just dissapear the way his mother and father did if he does anything to displease then was probably one of the factors that made genuine bonding difficult. It's one think to downplay his talent (which that statement already does sufficiently, "Ah, it's not that exceptional, I just kinda aquired this by necessity") and another to downright lie when there's no reason to. There's no reason why "Oh, it's just that I learned it at my Sensei's place" woudn't come off as significantly less humble, especially since he's basically saying the same thing - in fact, it would be more humble since it would imply having needed a longer time span to aquire his current skill level. We no longer know if the Cello thing happened in this continuity, but there's this 1.11 quote where Shinji displays a certain trust in that guy's word. (While Misato is driving him to headquarters.") Since he was living with a teacher , he may have been home schooled, but that's probably all there is to it/sufficient explanation. You know, not only religious nutjobs do this; It's also a way to provide ppl who can afford it with individual/best possible attention. That it IS a teacher (ie, someone whose job it is to look after children,) already says a lot about Gendo: He wanted Shinji to be in good hands, but failed to consider that a paid professional can only provide so much of an emotional connection. It would, for example, be highly improper for hugs to happen. He didn't just drop off Shinji at some orphanage, he made sure he was provided for (...allthough he'd probably have been better off with adopted parents/ the quality degree of professional caretaker only changed so much.), allthough Rebuild!Gendo, unlike his Series counterpart, didn#t really have the option to permanently get rid of him, since he planned to call him back and use him for the plan from the beginning... Still, Fuyu's quote seems to imply that the original plan was concoted when Yui was still around ("14 years ago"), and Yui... yeah, actually, it's hard to gauge how much she would allow and where things would end, but going by that flashback from 2.0, she did seem to want Gendo to look after him and mitigate the damage... and sending him off to live with that teacher guy is Gendo's version of doing that, or at least the closest he can manage with his limited abilities and skewed view of the affair. Yui was, to some degree, in on that plan. Probably not all of its specifics, since there's no way she could have forseen Rei's existence, but she may have planned for "the first candidate they find" to fill a certain role. As for the technology levels, it's highly imply that the developement of recreational electronics have been somewhat slowed by Second Impact, because they had to divert their ressources into things like the water restoration plants - The video game consoles (the ones Kensuke and Hikari have in eps 6 and 22 respectively) are also positively ancient. NERV has shiny phones because they're not just anyone, they're the organization fighting to preserve the planet; Cameras (especially after the general lawlessness that followed SI) and Phones are NOT recreational. This is a world whose people had to think very well what they invest their ressources in, because the oceans are ruined. Note that Shinji also owns this thing: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 11, 2013 4:47 pm @ Sailor Star Dust : I forgot to cite the conversation between Gendo and Fuyu about the "scenario planned 14 years ago", and it make me realize that it's possible that Yui was also part of this madness! ANd when you think about it, there are some hints left and right that she too took a part in the plan that would lead Shinji and Rei to awake EVA-01 : - first there is Gendo's treatment of Rei : unlike NGE where his relationship toward her was a blur, here he seems to really consider her as a daughter, hence the Ayanami surname, Yui's maiden name in Rebuild, meaning that Gendo made Rei a part of his family. Also he seems to be a little warmer toward her than in NGE, like when he dinned with her and accepted to participate in the dinner she will prepare for everyone. - speaking of their dinner, when Gendo saw a brief flash of Yui instead of Rei, her words(implied to be a memory) were rather strange : "Darling, please take care of Shinji." That strangely sounds like parting words, no? Why would she ask Gendo to care of Shinji with such a solemn tone? - in 3.0, Rei-Q's timing to attack was unnaturally perfect : just after Misato said that Rei "doesn't exist anymore", but before she had the time to explain what she meant. That makes me believes that Gendo have a way to spy on the Wunder, and even that he was monitoring Shinji's briefing to know exactly when making Mark.09 attack so Shinji would follow Rei-Q. As for the how he did it, if Yui's on Gendo's side, since she's now the Wunder's control system though EVA-01 she could have found a way to broadcast the briefing to Gendo! Rebuild already showed us that although many elements from NGE are also here, there is radical difference in key elements, like SEELE being actual immortal beings, HIP being nothing like the one in NGE, the presence of four ADAMs instead of one... So what if Gendo's Master Plan was actually also Yui's? What if they both planned that she will end in EVA-01, and that the soul of Lilith present inside it would be cast out during a salvage attempt, giving "birth" to Rei, and since they planned the arrival of this child, it's as if she's their second child whose "parents" lovingly planned to have one day! And what a glorious shock for both the audience and WILLE if it's revealed in FINAL that their ship of hope's control system is actually against them! As for Gendo's goals, the only thing we know is that one of the consequence will be that he will be reunited with Yui, that he needs an awakened Evangelion to carry it out(before it was planned to use EVA-01, after whatever happened during the time-skip that ended with Unit 01 in space he switched to EVA-13), and that the Key of N will play a part in it. And according to the preview for FINAL, we'll have another Impact and Instrumentality, so my guess is that Gendo plans to trigger HIP, only not the same version than SEELE, which would set his plan as similar than in NGE, so we're not safe from a surprise! EDIT : @ Kendrix : well, most of the theory is fanwankery, we won't probably know about the true extend of Gendo's manipulation until FINAL(if even there), most of the hint's could be interpreted as pure coincidence, but I just though that they could be connected to make a big foreshadowing of a possible cliffhanger(Yui and EVA-01 actually being on Gendo's side, something you seems to believes too! ) As for the brainwashing thing, I saw the scene again : - Fuyutsuki : and who will pilot Unit 01? - Gendo : The Dummy System isn't ready yet. Meanwhile, we'll do with the current pilot. - F : Brainwashing, if necessary? - G : And if we fail , there's still Rei. - F : Rei in Unit 01? Isn't that too risky?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 11, 2013 6:02 pm ElMariachi wrote: So yeah, making Rei pilot EVA-01 comes if they fail to make Shinji pilot, and that includes using brainwashing!

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Oct 11, 2013 7:26 pm ElMariachi wrote: @ Sailor Star Dust : I forgot to cite the conversation between Gendo and Fuyu about the "scenario planned 14 years ago", and it make me realize that it's possible that Yui was also part of this madness!
